page_header.label: SECURITY
page_header.title: Security Model
page_header.description: How ZKIRA protects your funds and privacy through battle-tested cryptography and self-custody architecture.

items.title: ECDH Key Exchange
items.description: Stealth addresses are derived using Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman key exchange. Sender's ephemeral key + receiver's view key = shared secret for one-time address generation.

items.title: SHA-256 Claim Verification
items.description: Claim secrets are verified on-chain using SHA-256 hashing. Only the hash is stored in escrow, preventing secret exposure while ensuring only valid claimants can unlock funds.

items.title: Ed25519 Signatures
items.description: All transactions use Solana's native Ed25519 signature scheme. Fast, secure, and deterministic signatures ensure transaction integrity without requiring trust in third parties.

items.title: Shielded Pool ZK Proofs
items.description: Withdrawals from the shielded pool require a Groth16 zero-knowledge proof that verifies knowledge of a valid deposit commitment without revealing which deposit is being claimed. Verified on-chain in under 200k compute units.

code.title: ZK proof verification
code.code:
proof = groth16.fullProve(input, wasm, zkey)
verify(proof, publicSignals, verificationKey)

items.title: Poseidon Merkle Tree
items.description: Deposit commitments are stored in a 20-level Poseidon Merkle tree. The ZK circuit proves membership in the tree without revealing the leaf position, providing cryptographic unlinkability between deposits and withdrawals.

items.title: Tor Hidden Service
items.description: RPC communications are routed through a Tor hidden service (.onion address), preventing network-level surveillance from correlating IP addresses with blockchain transactions.

items.title: Nym Mixnet
items.description: Browser clients use the Nym mixnet for packet-level privacy. Traffic is mixed with cover traffic and routed through multiple nodes, defeating even sophisticated network analysis.

sections.label: SELF-CUSTODY
sections.title: Your Keys, Your Funds
sections.subtitle: ZKIRA Pay operates as a non-custodial system. We never hold, touch, or have access to your private keys or funds.

items.title: Never Hold Private Keys
items.description: ZKIRA Pay smart contracts interact only with public addresses and signed transactions. Your private keys remain exclusively in your wallet, never transmitted or stored by our systems.

items.title: Deterministic Contracts
items.description: All escrow operations are governed by deterministic smart contracts. No human intervention, no admin overrides, no special privileges—just code executing exactly as programmed.

items.title: PDA-Based Escrow Accounts
items.description: Program Derived Addresses (PDAs) create deterministic escrow accounts. No private keys exist for these accounts—only the program can control them, eliminating human error or malicious access.

items.title: Time-Locked with Auto-Refund
items.description: Every escrow has a configurable expiry time. If unclaimed, funds automatically become refundable to the sender. No funds can ever be permanently lost or stuck in the system.

sections.label: OPEN SOURCE
sections.title: Verify Everything
sections.subtitle: True security requires transparency. Every line of ZKIRA Pay code is open source and auditable by anyone.

items.title: All Code Public on GitHub
items.description: Every smart contract, API endpoint, and frontend component is available on GitHub. No hidden code, no proprietary algorithms, no secret backdoors.

items.title: Verifiable Builds
items.description: Anchor programs are built deterministically. Anyone can verify that the deployed bytecode matches the published source code—no trust required.

sections.label: SECURITY DISCLOSURE
sections.title: Report Vulnerabilities
sections.subtitle: Security researchers and ethical hackers are essential to blockchain security. We welcome responsible disclosure of potential vulnerabilities.

paragraphs: Contact our security team at security@zkira.xyz. We follow responsible disclosure practices and will work with you to understand and address any security concerns.

items.title: REPORT
items.description: Send details to security@zkira.xyz with proof-of-concept

items.title: ACKNOWLEDGE
items.description: We confirm receipt and begin investigation within 48 hours

items.title: RESOLVE
items.description: Fix issued, coordinated disclosure, recognition provided

sections.title: Verify our claims
sections.description: Don't take our word for it. Examine our code, run your own tests, and verify every security claim we make.

sections.label: ATTACK RESISTANCE
sections.title: Forensic Defense Levels
sections.subtitle: Quantified resistance against blockchain forensics tools and surveillance adversaries.

items.title: ~95% vs Commercial Forensics
items.description: ZKIRA defeats Arkham Intelligence, Chainalysis, and Solscan through five overlapping defense layers: stealth addresses, shielded pool ZK proofs, timing defenses, network privacy, and data hygiene. The remaining ~5% gap comes from behavioral analysis patterns and small anonymity sets during early adoption — not cryptographic weakness.

items.title: ~70-80% vs Nation-State Adversaries
items.description: Against adversaries with global network monitoring capabilities, ZKIRA's Tor and Nym transport layers provide strong but not absolute protection. The binding constraint is anonymity set size — as adoption grows, resistance increases. The cryptographic primitives (ECDH, Groth16, Poseidon) are mathematically unbreakable regardless of adversary resources.

sections.label: MATHEMATICAL GUARANTEES
sections.title: Unbreakable Cryptography
sections.subtitle: The cryptographic foundations that make ZKIRA's privacy provably secure.

items.title: ECDH on Ed25519
items.description: Stealth addresses use the same elliptic curve cryptography securing billions in cryptocurrency. Breaking the sender-recipient link requires solving the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem — computationally infeasible with any known technology, including quantum computers at current scale.

items.title: Groth16 on BN254
items.description: Shielded pool withdrawals use Groth16 zero-knowledge proofs — the same proof system securing Zcash. The 20-level Poseidon Merkle tree with 11,354-constraint circuit provides cryptographic certainty that a valid deposit exists without revealing which one. Verification completes in under 200,000 Solana compute units.

items.title: Poseidon Hash Function
items.description: Purpose-built for ZK circuits, Poseidon operates natively over the BN254 scalar field. It provides 128-bit collision resistance while being 8x more efficient inside circuits than SHA-256 or Keccak. Each deposit commitment = Poseidon(nullifier, secret) is unique and unlinkable.

items.title: Timing Entropy
items.description: Poisson-distributed batch processing with λ=1 hour mean interval provides information-theoretic timing privacy. Combined with 6-hour soak time and automated decoy deposits every 30±10 minutes, timing analysis yields no statistically significant correlation between deposits and withdrawals.
